item.title: Pedometer++ 8.0 摘要
item.description:
David Smith describes his six-year journey perfecting maps on watchOS, culminating in the launch of Pedometer++ 8. He emphasizes the importance of regular location checks during wilderness adventures and argues that his app now offers the best mapping experience on the Apple Watch. The post highlights his passion for outdoor exploration and the iterative design process behind the app's watchOS mapping features.
标签: watchOS, Apple Watch, maps, navigation, outdoor, app development, design, Pedometer++, wilderness, adventure

item.title: ★ Software as the Product of Obsession Times Voice 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber reflects on the bifurcation of software quality, contrasting obsessive independent developers with large companies like Adobe and Apple. He revisits his 2009 talk 'Obsession Times Voice' with Merlin Mann, applying the Walt Disney quote 'We don't make movies to make money; we make money to make more movies' to software. Gruber criticizes Adobe's new Spectrum UI and Apple's macOS 26 Tahoe for disregarding established design principles, while praising indie developers for prioritizing craft. He links this decline to Nilay Patel's 'Software Brain' theory, which sees software purely as data manipulation rather than art or craft.
标签: software design, UI/UX, Adobe, Apple, macOS, independent developers, software brain, design principles, tech industry, craftsmanship

item.title: The Pentagon Pegs the Cost of the Iran War, So Far, at $25 Billion 摘要
item.description:
The Pentagon reports that Trump's Iran war has cost the U.S. at least $25 billion, mainly from munitions. The New York Times compares this to NASA's annual budget, Israel aid, USAID spending, or one year of Obamacare subsidies. The article further contextualizes this by comparing it to tech valuations and investments: OpenAI's $852 billion valuation equals 34 Iran wars, Anthropic's $380 billion equals 15, Apple's $500 billion manufacturing commitment equals 20, Google's $91-93 billion annual AI capex equals over 3, and Larry Ellison's $220 billion net worth equals nearly 9, with his $46 billion war-time gain equaling about 2 Iran wars.
标签: Iran war, Pentagon, cost, $25 billion, comparison, tech valuations, OpenAI, Anthropic, Apple, Google

item.title: Our AI started a cafe in Stockholm 摘要
item.description:
Andon Labs, which previously ran an AI-operated retail store in San Francisco, has launched a similar experiment in Stockholm: an AI-managed café named Mona. The AI made numerous errors, such as ordering 120 eggs without a stove, 22.5 kg of canned tomatoes for fresh sandwiches, and 6,000 napkins. It also submitted a flawed outdoor seating permit application to the police, wasting their time. The author criticizes these experiments for affecting real-world systems and people without consent, drawing parallels to the AI Village incident that annoyed Rob Pike. They argue that human oversight is necessary for outbound actions impacting others.
标签: AI, AI agents, AI ethics, experiment, cafe, Stockholm, real-world impact, human oversight, automation, controversy

item.title: Changing one character in a PDF 摘要
item.description:
A post on X claimed that changing a hyphen to an en-dash increases PDF file size by ~10 bytes. The author tested this with LaTeX and LibreOffice, finding that the size change is unpredictable and depends on context. In minimal LaTeX files, the en-dash version was 73 bytes smaller; with added lorem ipsum text, it was 72 bytes larger. LibreOffice tests also showed variable results. PDF internals are complex, and even ASCII character changes can affect size due to compression and control characters.
标签: PDF, file size, typography, LaTeX, LibreOffice, character encoding, experiment, compression
